LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS ABM INDUSTRIES, INC, ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES, INC, ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES - NC'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT TOOK THE MATTER UNDER SUBMISSION. (302/LMG) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI; REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998 - AFTER HEARING THE COURT RULED AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS' MSA AS TO THE 1ST COA IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT AS TO WHETHER BARBA WAS IN A SUPERVISORY POSITION AND AS TO WHETHER ABMJ-NC TOOK IMMEDIATE AND APPROPRIATE CORRECTIVE ACTION. FURTHER PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO THE REASONABLENESS OF HER ACTIONS IN USING THE PREVENTATIVE AND CORRECTIVE MEASURES THAT ABMJ-NC PROVIDE AND IF ABMJ-NC'S POLICIES AND PROCEDURES WERE PROPER. MOREOVER, THE "AVOIDABLE CONSEQUENCES" DOCTRINE LIMITS DAMAGES BUT NOT LIABILITY. DEFENDANTS' MSA ON THE 2ND COA IS GRANTED. LYDDAN CLEARLY PROVIDES THAT A FAILURE TO PREVENT HARASSMENT CLAIM DOES NOT GIVE RISE TO A PRIVATE CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANTS' MSA ON THE 3RD COA IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS PRESENTED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT THAT DEFENDANTS TOOK ADVERSE EMPLOYMENT ACTIONS (SIGNING CONFIDENTIALITY AGREEMENT, TRANSFER TO A POSITION THAT WAS TO LAST A YEAR BUT ONLY LAST SEVERAL MONTHS, FAILED TO TRANSFER HER BACK TO LONGER-LASTING POSITION ONCE BARBA WAS NO LONGER WORKING THERE) IN RESPONSE TO HER COMPLAINT. DEFENDANTS' MSA ON THE PUNITIVE DAMAGES CLAIM IS DENIED. ENTITLEMENT TO PUNITIVE DAMAGES IS GENERALLY AN ISSUE FOR THE TRIER OF FACT AND PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO DEFENDANTS' RATIFICATION OF BARBA'S ACTIONS BY KEEPING BARBA AT THE SAME PAY LEVEL, PROMOTING HIM BACK TO HIS FOREMAN POSITION AND NOT CONDUCTING A MEANINGFUL INVESTIGATION. DEFENDANTS ABNI AND ABMJ'S MSJ BECAUSE THEY NEVER EMPLOYED PLAINTIFF IS DENIED. PLAINTIFF HAS RAISED TRIABLE ISSUES OF FACT AS TO WHETHER THESE CORPORATIONS ARE, IN FACT, HER EMPLOYER. PARTIES NOTIFIED OF THE COURT'S RULING BY TELEPHONE.

Off Calendar Sep-08-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANTS ABM INDUSTRIES, INCORPORATED, ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES, INC., ABM JANITORIAL SERVICES - NORTHERN CALIFORNIA MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Jennifer A. Reisch of Talamantes Villegas Carrera LLP (989.8000) and Monali Sheth of Equal Rights Advocates (621.0672) for plaintiff and responding party; Matthew D. Marca and Alexis Sohrakoff of Littler Mendelson PC (399.8474) for defendants and moving parties. Argued and the court took the matter under submission. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi; Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998 --- After hearing the court ruled as follows: Defendants' motion for summary adjudication as to the 1st cause of action is denied. Plaintiff has raised a triable issue of fact as to whether Barba was in a supervisory position and as to whether ABMJ-NC took immediate and appropriate corrective action. Further plaintiff has raised triable issues of fact as to the reasonableness of her actions in using the preventative and corrective measures that ABMJ-NC provide and if ABMJ-NC's policies and procedures were proper. Moreover, the "avoidable consequences" doctrine limits damages but not liability. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication on the 2nd cause of action is granted. Lyddan clearly provides that a failure to prevent harassment claim does not give rise to a private cause of action. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication on the 3rd cause of action is denied. Plaintiff has presented triable issues of fact that defendants took adverse employment actions (signing confidentiality agreement, transfer to a position that was to last a year but only last several months, failed to transfer her back to longer-lasting position once Barba was no longer working there) in response to her complaint. Defendants' motion for summary adjudication on the punitive damages claim is denied. Entitlement to punitive damages is generally an issue for the trier of fact and plaintiff has raised triable issues of fact as to defendants' ratification of Barba's actions by keeping Barba at the same pay level, promoting him back to his foreman position and not conducting a meaningful investigation. Defendants ABNI and ABMJ's motion for summary judgment because they never employed plaintiff is denied. Plaintiff has raised triable issues of fact as to whether these corporations are, in fact, her employer. Parties notified of the court's ruling by telephone.
